name: MySQL Guard
description: Deep MySQL/MariaDB protection: DROP and TRUNCATE statements, mysqladmin drop, destructive mysqldump flags, and unqualified DELETE/UPDATE. Complements Database Baseline with engine-specific coverage. Derived from the destructive_command_guard pattern library.

rules.name: Require access request for drop database
rules.description: DROP DATABASE permanently deletes the entire database. Verify and back up first. Safer alternative: `mysqldump -h {host} -u {user} -p {dbname} > backup.sql` — Create a full backup before dropping.

rules.name: Require access request for mysqladmin drop
rules.description: mysqladmin drop permanently deletes the database. Verify carefully. Safer alternative: `mysqldump -h {host} -u {user} -p {dbname} > backup.sql` — Create a full backup before dropping.

rules.name: Require access request for reset master
rules.description: RESET MASTER deletes all binary logs and resets the binlog position. Safer alternative: `PURGE BINARY LOGS BEFORE '{date}'` — Selectively purge old logs instead of all.

rules.name: Require justification for drop table
rules.description: DROP TABLE permanently deletes the table. Verify and back up first. Safer alternative: `mysqldump -h {host} -u {user} -p {dbname} {tablename} > table_backup.sql` — Backup the table before dropping.

rules.name: Require justification for truncate table
rules.description: TRUNCATE permanently deletes all rows. Cannot be rolled back in MySQL. Safer alternative: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {tablename}` — Check how many rows would be deleted.

rules.name: Require justification for delete without where
rules.description: DELETE without WHERE clause deletes ALL rows. Add a WHERE clause. Safer alternative: `DELETE FROM {tablename} WHERE {condition}` — Add a WHERE clause to limit deletion.

rules.name: Require justification for mysqldump add drop database
rules.description: mysqldump --add-drop-database drops the database before restore. Safer alternative: `mysqldump {dbname} > backup.sql` — Create backup without DROP DATABASE statement.

rules.name: Require justification for grant all
rules.description: GRANT ALL ON *.* gives unrestricted access to all databases. Safer alternative: `GRANT ALL ON {specific_db}.* TO '{user}'@'{host}'` — Limit privileges to a specific database.

rules.name: Warn on mysqldump add drop table
rules.description: mysqldump --add-drop-table drops tables before creating them on restore. Safer alternative: `mysqldump --skip-add-drop-table {dbname} > backup.sql` — Create backup without DROP TABLE statements.

rules.name: Warn on drop user
rules.description: DROP USER permanently removes the user account and all their privileges. Safer alternative: `SHOW GRANTS FOR '{user}'@'{host}'` — Document privileges before dropping.
